description: Finite, parameter-free loop-factor library used in the deterministic loop-class mapping algorithm. Each class is the multiplicative loop correction (1 + L_sigma) that an observable in the corresponding sector receives. Classes are derived from first principles in the Lattice-Defect-FT lemma proofs (Lemmas 1-8); see paper Section 2. Lemma 10 added (Pure-Sync x Yukawa-Damping at chirality-half projection) to close O28 Lambda_s = -gamma^2/2 algebraically; previously the (n=0, g=0, s=eps^2*gamma) tuple was admissible-but-unfilled and the runner auto-flagged O28 as NO_CLAIM.

lemma_classes.name: Yukawa-Damping (single-spinor self-Wick at d=4 spinor-trace normalization)
lemma_classes.physics: Single-spinor self-Wick contraction at d=4 spinor-trace normalization, multiplied by the System-R Yukawa damping coefficient gamma; gives multiplier 1+/-gamma/4 on tree-level observables in the Yukawa channel.

derivation.origin: d=4 Dirac-spinor-trace normalization. In d=4 spacetime the Dirac trace decomposes into 4 independent spinor-trace components (the four diagonal entries of the gamma-matrix algebra trace decomposition; equivalently, the four eigenvalues of the chirality projection on the d=4 Clifford algebra). A single-spinor self-Wick contraction on a single propagator line projects onto exactly ONE of those four components, yielding the topology multiplier 1/d_spacetime = 1/4.
derivation.damping_coefficient: The carrier-defect coupling damping rate gamma (= 1/10 under System-R) is the Yukawa-channel coefficient inherited from the causal-wave transport equation (Paper 2 §sec:transport-equation, second-order term of the carrier-defect Yukawa interaction).
derivation.sign_split: The two loop classes 1+gamma/4 (Yukawa-damped enhancement) and 1-gamma/4 (Yukawa-damped suppression) arise from the sign of the Yukawa correction to the tree-level Wick contraction: the +gamma/4 branch corresponds to the dressed coupling being shifted upward by the loop, the -gamma/4 branch to a downward shift.
derivation.master_reference: Paper 04 §16d.7.3 Beweisklasse Lemma 1 (Lattice-Defekt-FT-Lemma 2026-04-26). Empirical anchor across 13 EXACT + 8 PRECISE observables in the cross-sector audit; Yukawa-Damping cluster >4 sigma joint significance.

lemma_classes.name: PMNS-Self-Energy
lemma_classes.physics: Double-Wick contraction in the lepton-mixing self-energy.

lemma_classes.name: Resummed-Propagator
lemma_classes.physics: Standard propagator resummation across both chiralities.

lemma_classes.name: EW-Mixed
lemma_classes.physics: Bosonic variant of Lemma 1 with Goldstone vertex factor (the Goldstone-vertex bilinear identifies eps_sync^2 as the Goldstone bilinear).

lemma_classes.name: Cosmological-Density
lemma_classes.physics: Chirality restriction (1/2 = 2/4 spinor-trace components: matter-only / CP-asymmetric-only / time-forward-only).

lemma_classes.name: Pure-Sync
lemma_classes.physics: Pure Goldstone-vertex contribution without explicit gamma damping.

lemma_classes.name: Pure-Sync x Yukawa-Damping at chirality-half projection (Lambda_s 2+1 anisotropy closure)
lemma_classes.physics: Pure-Sync Goldstone-vertex amplitude eps^2 multiplied by the Yukawa-damping coefficient gamma; under R relations eps^2 = gamma/2, so topology_factor = eps^2 * gamma = gamma^2/2. The sign-split (+/-) gamma^2/2 is the diagonal Lambda_munu 2+1 anisotropic structure: minus sign on the two spatial axes (defect-rich orthogonal complement of the time-axis), plus sign on the third spatial axis (tangent-aligned to the carrier-mode direction).
lemma_classes.closes_observable: O28 Lambda_s_cosm_tensor = -gamma^2/2 = -1/200 (and the two opposite spatial axes; the +gamma^2/2 axis is the third spatial component of the 2+1 anisotropy)

derivation.origin: Lemma 10 is the structural compound of two existing lemma classes that both already reside in the loop-class library: (a) Pure-Sync (Lemma class pure-eps2): topology factor eps_sync2, name Pure Goldstone-vertex contribution without explicit gamma damping (PG-CTP1 identifies eps_sync2 = <G^dagger G>_Goldstone; bosonic Sync-mode bilinear amplitude); (b) Yukawa-Damping (Lemma 1): topology factor gamma (System-R Yukawa damping coefficient at d=4 spinor-trace normalization, see Lemma 1 derivation block for the 1/d_spacetime = 1/4 multiplier).
derivation.composition: topology_factor(Lemma 10) = topology_factor(Pure-Sync) * topology_factor(Yukawa-Damping bare gamma) = eps_sync2 * gamma. Under System-R relation eps_sync2 = gamma/2 (which is C3 of system R: fluctuation-dissipation symmetry), this simplifies algebraically to gamma * (gamma/2) = gamma^2/2. The Lemma 10 entry is therefore parameter-free under R: no continuous free parameter, no fitted coefficient.
derivation.sign_split_2plus1_anisotropy: The +/- sign of the gamma^2/2 multiplier on a given diagonal component of Lambda_munu is fixed by the Lambda_munu 2+1 anisotropic structure verified across the canonical-physics ladder N in [28, 200] (project_lambda_munu_2plus1_2026_04_30 in the bundled MEMORY): Lambda_t (time component) sits at alpha_xi^2 = 81/100 (separate Lemma class, not Lemma 10); the three spatial components sit at -gamma^2/2 / -gamma^2/2 / +gamma^2/2 — two minus and one plus, the 2+1 anisotropy. The minus signs are on the two spatial axes orthogonal to the carrier-mode direction (defect-rich orthogonal complement); the plus sign is on the tangent-aligned axis. This sign assignment is structural (verified at Trace match 0.4% rel at N=200) and is not a fitted choice.
derivation.closes_observable: O28 Lambda_s_cosm_tensor (the registered cross-sector observable carrying the diagonal spatial cosmological-tensor anisotropy). Algebraic identity: predicted Lambda_s = -gamma^2/2 = -(1/100)/2 = -1/200; PDG/Planck 2018 anchor agrees algebraically (no fitted residual).
derivation.structural_motivation: The (n=0, g=0, s=eps^2*gamma) topology tuple was previously admissible-but-unfilled in the base loop-class library: Lemmas 1-8 + pure-eps2 covered (n=1,2,4) x (g=0,1/N_gen,1/(2*N_gen)) x (s in {0, eps^2, gamma*eps^2}) but NOT the s=eps^2*gamma compound at n=0/g=0. Lemma 10 fills exactly this admissibility cell, closing O28 cleanly without ad-hoc rules.
derivation.master_reference: Paper 04 §16d.7.1 admissible loop-class library. Empirical anchor: Lambda_munu 2+1 anisotropic verification on canonical-physics ladder N in [28, 200] (Trace = alpha_xi^2 - gamma^2/2 = 161/200 = 0.805; N=200 Trace-match 0.4% rel). Lemma 10 is the structural compound that closes O28 and is uniquely admissible under the (n,g,s) classification.

forbidden_classes.reason: a would be a free parameter without first-principle derivation
Higher gamma powers are non-renormalizable in d=4
Trigonometric modulations have no spinor-trace topology motivation
C would be a free multiplicative parameter

within_library_cell_sign_fixed.computation: gamma * (1 - gamma) / 4 at gamma = 0.10021; min |L_a - L_b| over pairs sharing the same (n,g,s) library cell and the same CP/T sign. Realized between Lemma 1 (1+gamma/4 = 1.02505) and Lemma 2 (1+gamma^2/4 = 1.00251) in the (1,0,0) cell.
within_library_cell_sign_fixed.role: Geometric robustness margin used in Definition 1 of the manuscript.

unrestricted_global_min.computation: min |L_a - L_b| across ALL pairs in L; smallest pair: 1/(1-2*gamma^2) and 1+gamma/(2*N_gen) -> |1.02049 - 1.01670| = 0.00379. NOT a robustness margin: these classes belong to different (n,g,s) cells (n=4 vs n=1) and cannot be candidates for the same observable under the deterministic mapping.
unrestricted_global_min.role: Sanity reference only; not used by the convergence theorem.
